project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.
current_milestone: M2 — Evidence Log skeleton (S3.1 aios-evidence crate)
current_task_title: T-014 closed — privacy ceiling on Query/Subscribe per S3.1 §10 + §23.2 + §11.4
current_step: T-014 closed. Privacy ceiling on Query + Subscribe per S3.1 §10 + §23.2 + §11.4 landed. New module crates/aios-evidence/src/privacy.rs (~445 LoC) with PrivacyCeiling struct (subject_canonical_id, primary_group, is_ai, is_recovery_mode), admits()/admits_wire() closed predicates implementing 6 spec rules in priority order: (1) system caller (empty subject) admits all — test-only backward-compat shortcut documented as future-hardened; (2) self-receipt always admitted; (3) constitutionally-public record types (PolicyDecision/RecoveryEvent/TamperDetected/SegmentSealed/ChainCheckpoint/FirstBoot*/Bundle*/Override*/etc — closed match list of 42 RecordTypes) admitted to non-AI callers; (4) group-scoped admission for non-AI callers per §23.2; (5) AI strictness — AI callers see only own + public-about-system records, never other AI subjects' records, even in recovery mode; (6) recovery-mode broadening — non-AI operators in recovery_mode see all FOREVER-retention receipts across groups (§11.4 forensic access). Receipt's primary group derived from rev.2 provisional grammar 'type:group/name' via subject_primary_group() until S5.1 NamespaceScope wiring lands (carry-forward documented). receipt_subject_is_system() detects _system:* actors. Proto additive fields (T-014, S3.1 §10 + §23.2): QueryRequest +caller_primary_group(10)/caller_is_ai(11)/caller_is_recovery_mode(12) — spec's existing `subject = 9` is the caller canonical id; SubscribeRequest +caller_subject(6)/caller_primary_group(7)/caller_is_ai(8)/caller_is_recovery_mode(9). Proto3-additive = wire-compatible with T-011..T-013 traffic. Suppressed-count surfaced via `x-aios-suppressed-count` initial-metadata header (tonic 0.12 lacks trailing-metadata on success path; documented choice — same client-visible semantics). InMemoryEvidenceLog + RocksDbEvidenceLog Query/Subscribe handlers wired identically — ceiling.admits() filters historic + replay; ceiling.admits_wire() filters live broadcast events. New helpers optional_group() + attach_suppressed_count() in both backend files. Tests: 17 unit tests in privacy.rs (system-caller bypass, self-receipt, public records to non-AI/AI, group same/cross/no-declared, AI isolation in groups, recovery broadening for FOREVER only + not for STANDARD + not for AI, subject_primary_group grammar, receipt_subject_is_system underscore detection, is_constitutionally_public_record_type sample membership). 5 service-level Query tests in impl_inmemory.rs (admits self+filters others; public records pass-through; group admission; backward-compat empty subject; AI isolation). 2 integration tests in tests/privacy_ceiling.rs (3-subject corpus through gRPC Query: operator-1@ops/operator-2@finance/ai-1@ops + system records, all 4 caller personas asserted; subscribe replay filters). Backward compat preserved: existing tests' empty `subject` field selects system caller (admits all) — T-007..T-013 wire-compat untouched. 8 existing tests updated with new caller_* fields (default values). Workspace 306 → 330 tests (+24). Cycles: cargo build, cargo test --workspace (330 PASS), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS), cargo fmt --all -- --check (PASS).
completed_steps_M1: T-001 .. T-006 — M1 closed at 102 tests
completed_steps_M2: T-007 .. T-013 — see prior memory snapshot

T-014: privacy ceiling on Query + Subscribe per S3.1 §10 + §23.2 + §11.4. New file crates/aios-evidence/src/privacy.rs (~445 LoC). PrivacyCeiling struct + admits()/admits_wire() closed predicates implementing 6 rules in priority order (system-caller bypass; self-receipt; constitutionally-public records to non-AI; group-scoped; AI strictness; recovery-mode FOREVER broadening). Closed list of 42 constitutionally-public RecordTypes (PolicyDecision/RecoveryEvent/TamperDetected/SegmentSealed/ChainCheckpoint/EmergencyOverrideGrant/PolicyBundleLoad/SystemAdminOperation/CrossGroupAccessDenied/Recovery*x8/FirstBoot*x6/Override*x8/Bundle*x6). subject_primary_group() extracts group from rev.2 'type:group/name' grammar (carry-forward to S5.1 NamespaceScope). receipt_subject_is_system() detects _system:* prefix. Proto3-additive schema changes: QueryRequest +fields 10/11/12 (caller_primary_group/is_ai/is_recovery_mode; spec's `subject=9` is the caller canonical id, not a content filter), SubscribeRequest +fields 6/7/8/9 (caller_subject/_primary_group/_is_ai/_is_recovery_mode). Wire-compatible with T-011..T-013 traffic. Suppressed-count emitted as `x-aios-suppressed-count` initial-metadata header — tonic 0.12 has no trailing-metadata-on-success API and the count is known at response-construct time (full hits Vec materialized); documented design choice. Both InMemoryEvidenceLog and RocksDbEvidenceLog Query/Subscribe paths updated symmetrically. Backward compat: empty `subject` → PrivacyCeiling::system_caller() admits all (test-only shortcut documented as future hardened in T-016/M3). 8 existing test fixtures updated with new caller_* default fields. 17 unit tests in privacy.rs + 5 service-level Query tests in impl_inmemory.rs + 2 integration tests in tests/privacy_ceiling.rs covering: per-caller visibility, suppressed_count correctness, recovery mode broadening, AI isolation, group scoping. Workspace 306 → 330 (+24). All gates: cargo build --workspace + cargo test --workspace (330 PASS) + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS) + cargo fmt --all -- --check (PASS).
discovered_constraints_T014: S3.1 §10 IDL already prescribes QueryRequest.subject (field 9) as the caller-identity field for privacy ceiling (NOT a content subject filter). The existing field is reused as-is for the caller canonical id; the richer NormalizedSubject projection (primary_group + is_ai + is_recovery_mode) is added as additive proto3 fields 10..12 (wire-compatible).

Spec §10 says 'count returned in the stream trailer'. tonic 0.12 server-streaming has no trailing-metadata-on-success API — the only path is Status::with_metadata on the final ending Status, which would require breaking the stream-from-vec pattern. The pragmatic choice: emit the count via initial metadata header `x-aios-suppressed-count` (clients read identically via Response::metadata()). Documented in attach_suppressed_count() helper. When Query later switches to a true streaming-from-disk implementation, the helper can switch to a Status trailer in the tail-of-stream Status.

PrivacyCeiling::admits() needs receipt.subject_canonical_id() + receipt.record_type() only. To filter live broadcast events (where the sealed receipt is not at hand), the admits_wire(subject:&str, rt: RecordType) variant operates on the wire-form projection — avoids resurrecting the sealed receipt from the chain inside the broadcast filter_map closure (which would need to acquire the async RwLock per event and is hostile to tokio's filter_map signature).

clippy::significant_drop_tightening fires on tonic Channel clients held across multiple awaits in integration tests; existing tests/redaction_roundtrip.rs handles it with #![allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening, reason='...')]. Applied the same pattern in tests/privacy_ceiling.rs.

Spec §23.2 'suppressed_count' is the wire name; emitted under the tonic metadata key SUPPRESSED_COUNT_TRAILER ('x-aios-suppressed-count'). Exported from service::mod and re-exported at crate root so external clients can read it without reaching into service::*.

Group derivation from the rev.2 provisional subject canonical-id grammar 'type:group/name' is the carry-forward T-014 path until S5.1 lands a typed NamespaceScope on the receipt envelope. Documented at subject_primary_group() — when the typed field arrives, the helper will read it directly instead of string-splitting.

Constitutionally-public RecordTypes list (42 entries in is_constitutionally_public_record_type()) is closed: spec §13 FOREVER-retention bucket + system-audit families + Recovery* + FirstBoot* + Override* + Bundle*. Operators see all of these; AI callers see only the ones whose subject is _system:* (§11.4 + S5.1 AI rule). Listed by name with category comments to ease future audits.
notes: T-014 closed. T-015 (compaction worker per S3.1 §12 / §13) queued next — final task before M2 honest closure.
